module.name: eval
module.spec:
Overview: eval/main.go is the standalone entry point for the eval harness, invoked via `go run ./eval [flags] [prompt]`. It parses flags, constructs an eval pipeline, and dispatches either a single-prompt run or a battery run across multiple prompts in parallel.
Defined terms: subject — the LLM being tested (drives drift against a fixture workspace) judge — the LLM scoring the subject's output battery — running all (or N) prompts under eval/prompts/ instead of one explicit prompt repeat — running the same prompt N times for statistical baseline
Requirements: R1. main MUST accept flags: --subject, --judge, --battery, --only, --runs, --repeat, --label, --dry-run, --skip-judge. R2. --only MUST imply --battery and MUST accept a comma-separated list of prompt names; only those prompts run. R3. --runs MUST be capped by the number of available prompts when --battery is set. R4. --dry-run MUST stage the run (write workspace setup) but skip LLM calls. R5. --skip-judge MUST run the subject pass but skip the judge pass; subject transcripts MUST still be written. R6. When no prompt is supplied positionally and --battery/--only is not set, main MUST print usage to stderr and exit non-zero. R7. Per-prompt overrides via <name>-subject.md and <name>-judge.md files alongside <name>.md MUST be honored when present.
See also: eval.pipeline (when specced).
